on public policies. It captures direct and indirect impacts to reflect the full spectrum of climate action.\"},{\"question\":\"How does the CCF ensure fair comparison across sectors?\",\"answer\":\"The CCF applies context-adjusted weighting according to business sector, geography, company size, and profit margins. High-impact sectors are assessed on broader contribution ranges. This approach enables peer comparisons while reflecting structurally different roles in the transition.\"},{\"question\":\"What is the relationship between the CCF and existing frameworks?\",\"answer\":\"The CCF is designed to complement (not replace) frameworks like SBTi or the GHG Protocol. It fills current gaps by integrating indirect impacts and climate finance, aligning with best practice methodologies, and providing a unified benchmark covering multiple frameworks.\"},{\"question\":\"Who develops the CCF?\",\"answer\":\"The initiative is led by Sweep and the Mirova Research Center. Methodological development is carried out with I Care by BearingPoint and Winrock International. A group of sponsoring companies supports the project, and an independent advisory committee composed of experts from leading climate organizations provides oversight.\"},{\"question\":\"What is the relationship between the CCF and the Sweep platform?\",\"answer\":\"The CCF defines the contribution assessment methodology. Sweep provides the technology to operationalize it: data collection and consolidation, methodology application, calculation of the three indicators, scenario simulation, and action planning. The framework sets the rules, Sweep provides the digital engine.\"},{\"question\":\"What is the long-term ambition of the CCF?\",\"answer\":\"To establish the CCF as the global reference for corporate climate contribution, by integrating and building on existing frameworks. Ultimately, the goal is to influence public policies so that every company is required to define and publish a Contribution Plan.\"},{\"question\":\"Is the CCF publicly accessible?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The CCF is designed to be accessible to all, its development and dissemination being funded by philanthropy.
